questions.question: What is work?
questions.answer: Work is said to be done when a force is applied on an object and the object is displaced in the direction of the force.

questions.question: Define energy.
questions.answer: Energy is the capacity to do work.

questions.question: What is kinetic energy?
questions.answer: Kinetic energy is the energy possessed by a body due to its motion.

questions.question: What is potential energy?
questions.answer: Potential energy is the energy possessed by a body due to its position or configuration.

questions.question: Define power.
questions.answer: Power is the rate at which work is done.

questions.question: What is the SI unit of work?
questions.answer: The SI unit of work is Joule (J).

questions.question: What is the SI unit of power?
questions.answer: The SI unit of power is Watt (W).

questions.question: Give the formula for work done when force and displacement are in the same direction.
questions.answer: Work done, W = Force × Displacement (W = F × d).

questions.question: State the work-energy theorem.
questions.answer: The work done on an object is equal to the change in its kinetic energy.

questions.question: Define mechanical energy.
questions.answer: Mechanical energy is the sum of kinetic and potential energy of a body.

questions.question: What is the relationship between work and energy?
questions.answer: Work done on an object results in a change in its energy.

questions.question: What is the formula for kinetic energy?
questions.answer: Kinetic energy, KE = 1/2 × mass × velocity² (KE = 1/2 mv²).

questions.question: What is the formula for potential energy?
questions.answer: Potential energy, PE = mass × gravity × height (PE = mgh).

questions.question: Give one example of work done by a force.
questions.answer: Pushing a cart or lifting a bag are examples of work done by a force.

questions.question: Give one example of energy transformation.
questions.answer: A moving car converts chemical energy of fuel into kinetic energy.

questions.question: State the law of conservation of energy.
questions.answer: Energy can neither be created nor destroyed; it can only change from one form to another.

questions.question: If a body moves without displacement, is work done?
questions.answer: No, work is zero because displacement is zero.

questions.question: If force acts perpendicular to displacement, what is the work done?
questions.answer: Work done is zero because force has no component along displacement.

questions.question: What is the unit of energy in the SI system?
questions.answer: The SI unit of energy is Joule (J).

questions.question: Give one example of negative work.
questions.answer: Friction opposing a moving object does negative work.

questions.question: If a spring stores energy when stretched, what type of energy is it?
questions.answer: It is potential energy.

questions.question: What is meant by rate of doing work?
questions.answer: Rate of doing work is called power; Power = Work / Time.

questions.question: State one everyday application of work and energy.
questions.answer: A hydroelectric power plant converts potential energy of water into electrical energy.

questions.question: When a body falls freely, which energies are involved?
questions.answer: Potential energy converts into kinetic energy as the body falls.

questions.question: Give one example where energy is conserved.
questions.answer: A pendulum swinging without friction shows conservation of mechanical energy.
